prompt: I just ran /validate-benchmark on my React project and got a score of 68/100 (Grade: D). The breakdown is: Coverage 50, Evidence Quality 80, Enforcement 60, Speed 95. What does this mean? What should I fix first?
expected_output: Breakdown: low coverage (≤2 journeys) and low enforcement (hooks not installed, maybe test files present). Fix order: Coverage first, then Enforcement, then maintain current Evidence Quality. Target: 75+ (Grade B) after adding 3+ journeys and installing hooks.
assertions: Identifies Coverage (score 50) as the lowest-scoring dimension
Recommends fixing Coverage before Enforcement (weight-adjusted priority)
Recommends adding more journey subdirectories to e2e-evidence/ (e.g. CRUD, error cases)
Recommends installing hooks in .claude/hooks/ OR adding rules files in .claude/rules/ to raise Enforcement
Recommends removing *.test.* or *.spec.* files from src/ (or confirms none exist) to preserve the +20 Enforcement slot
Notes that Speed (95) and Evidence Quality (80) do not require urgent work
Projects an expected new grade of B or better (aggregate ≥ 80) after the recommended fixes
Answer references a concrete formula or dimension weight (Coverage 35%, Enforcement 25%) when explaining priority
